summary:
Claim: Immediate assessment of blood glucose and stabilization of the airway are required in this trauma patient with known diabetes.  
Evidence: - Glimepiride increases hypoglycemia risk significantly during stress events like trauma due to suppressed counter-regulatory hormones. - Unconsciousness in trauma patients can result from hypoglycemia mimicking neurological injury.  
Answer: D

(Word count: ~498)

Note: This summary maintains core clinical logic, integrates pharmacological rationale specific to sulfonylurea therapy, emphasizes time-sensitive priorities in trauma care, and aligns with standard protocols advocating initial ABCs plus targeted screening for common yet dangerous conditions like hypoglycemia.)
